overview: The collaboration and networking area covers how institutions form partnerships and alliances to support digitalisation and AI adoption. It includes structured cooperation with other universities, engagement with industry and public bodies, and participation in European and international consortia. Effective networking provides access to shared resources, joint curricula, and professional development opportunities, while also ensuring that new technologies are integrated inclusively across sectors. Collaboration is both a strategic tool and a cultural practice, reducing duplication and embedding innovation within a broader ecosystem.

instructions.maturity_scoring: For each item/statement in the following table, select: [0] Not in place | [1] Partially in place | [2] Fully in place providing also clear evidence for the suggested score and owner responsible for this item. Examples for evidence and owner are provided within the table below. Note that items which are marked as Critical need to be implemented before approaching to higher maturity level (necessary/minimum conditions).

items.item: Existence of formal partnerships with HEIs on digitalisation and AI

score_anchors.1: Some partnerships exist but are informal or limited in scope.
score_anchors.2: Multiple formal partnerships with clear agreements and active collaboration.

items.evidence_examples: Signed MoUs, partnership agreements, records of joint activities

items.item: Participation in national or EU- level digitalisation and AI networks

score_anchors.2: Regular, structured participation with defined roles.

items.evidence_examples: Membership lists, meeting minutes, network communicati ons

items.item: Structured engagement with industry and community partners

score_anchors.2: Ongoing, structured engagement with clear outcomes.

items.evidence_examples: Industry partnership agreements, event records, internship data

items.item: Shared infrastructure, platforms, or resources across institutions
items.owners: IT Services and Digital Learning Centre

items.item: Faculty and student mobility or exchange opportunities in AI and digitalisation
Dissemination of outcomes through joint events, publications, or platforms
items.evidence_examples: Event calendars, publication lists, platform analytics
items.owners: Centre for Continuing Education and Lifelong Learning

items.item: Recognition of collaborative achievements in institutional evaluations

score_anchors.2: Systematic inclusion in evaluation and promotion processes.

items.evidence_examples: Evaluation guidelines, promotion criteria, review documentatio n
items.owners: Vice- Rector for Research and Innovation

score_ranges.interpretation: Collaboration on AI and digitalisation is fragmented and project-based. No stable partnerships or structured exchanges exist, and outcomes are rarely shared institutionally.

Formal partnerships and network participation are in place, though limited in scope or consistency. Joint projects, exchanges, and dissemination activities occur regularly but remain dependent on individual initiatives.

Collaboration is strategic and institutionalised. The university maintains active national and international partnerships, shared infrastructure, and regular joint outputs. Cooperation is embedded in governance and staff evaluation processes.

build_action_plan.intro: Before progressing to a higher level of maturity within the Collaboration and Networking dimension of the AI DTP framework, it is essential that all critical items identified in section 3.6.2 are fully implemented. For each critical item, the corresponding necessary actions must be clearly defined and executed (see Table below).

foundational_actions.action: Formalise at least one partnership agreement with another HEI or industry partner in digitalisation and AI.
Join or initiate participation in at least one national or EU-level digitalisation and AI network.
Establish a structured engagement process with at least one industry or community partner.
Provide access to at least one shared digital or AI infrastructure/platform with a partner institution.
foundational_actions.owners: IT Services and Digital Learning Centre

foundational_actions.action: Update institutional evaluation and promotion guidelines to recognise collaborative achievements.

Emerging.action: Map existing partnerships and identify potential partners in AI and digitalisation.
Sign initial cooperation agreements with other HEIs or industry partners.
Organise joint pilot activities such as workshops or seminars to explore opportunities.
Establish internal coordination points for international collaboration.

Established.action: Expand collaborative activities into joint teaching resources and shared curricula.
Formalise governance structures for managing partnerships and joint projects.
Launch mobility opportunities for students and staff in AI and digitalisation.
Integrate industry partners into projects through internships, mentorship, or case studies.
Established.owners: Centre for Continuing Education and Lifelong Learning

Established.action: Disseminate outcomes through joint events, publications, and shared platforms.
Established.owners: Centre for Continuing Education and Lifelong Learning

Enhanced.action: Lead or co-lead international alliances and consortia on AI and digitalisation.
Develop joint degrees, micro-credentials, or lifelong learning programmes across institutions.
Enhanced.owners: Centre for Continuing Education and Lifelong Learning

Enhanced.action: Establish shared infrastructure or innovation hubs with partner institutions and industry.
Enhanced.owners: IT Services and Digital Learning Centre

Enhanced.action: Secure joint funding through European or international programmes.
Embed collaboration outcomes into institutional evaluation and promotion processes.

kpis.intro: The following table presents key performance indicators (KPIs) for this area and target values required to achieve each maturity level. For each KPI, the corresponding owner should be specified.

items.name: Number of active institutional partnerships in digitalisation and AI
Percentage of faculties participating in at least one joint AI/digitalisation initiative per year
Number of joint outputs (co- developed courses, co- organized events, co- authored publications) per year
items.owners: Centre for Continuing Education and Lifelong Learning

items.name: Percentage of students in relevant programmes (e.g. AI, digitalisation, related fields) participating in physical or virtual cross-institutional exchanges per year
Number of industry or public sector partners in collaborative projects
Proportion of partnerships linked to international consortia (%)
Percentage of faculty and staff participating in at least one joint (cross- institutional or cross- sector) training or professional development activity in AI/digitalisation per year
items.owners: Centre for Continuing Education and Lifelong Learning

items.name: Number of shared digital or AI infrastructure platforms operated jointly with partner institutions or industry (e.g. shared LMS instances, joint sandboxes, shared cloud environments)
items.owners: IT Services and Digital Learning Centre

items.name: Recognition of collaboration outcomes (in AI and digitalization) in faculty evaluation and promotion processes (%)
Number of co-authored publications or joint funding applications related to AI and digitalisation (per year)
